reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 2 (Trust Boundary): This finding requires the Engine API (semi-trusted, JWT-authenticated EL-CL communication) to return malformed data. Per BUG_BOUNTY_SCOPE.json lines 107-118, Engine API is semi-trusted and vulnerabilities are in-scope but 'attacks requiring a compromised local EL may be considered conditional or lower severity.' The attack scenario describes a 'c-kzg-4844 library bug or version mismatch' causing ComputeCells() to return ≠128 cells—this is not an attacker-controlled input but rather a code-intrinsic library behavior issue. The code does construct exactly 128 proofs (via numberOfColumns loop at L156) while accepting whatever cell count ComputeCells() returns, creating a potential mismatch. However, this requires either (a) a compromised/buggy EL returning malformed data, or (b) a c-kzg library version mismatch—both outside the threat model for untrusted P2P peers. 01e reference: outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W3B4_1771748827.json, PROP-57888860-inv-027 (lines 680-696): 'Each blob must produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cell-proof pairs in cells_and_kzg_proofs construction.' The code reading is factually correct (no validation at L150-153), but the attack path depends on a compromised or buggy semi-trusted component (EL via Engine API), not untrusted input.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: Each blob must produce exactly CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB cell-proof pairs in cells_and_kzg_proofs construction.
